title: STANDARD FOR DEGERMED MAIZE (CORN) MEAL AND MAIZE (CORN) GRITS

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DEGERMED MAIZE (CORN) MEAL AND MAIZE (CORN) GRITS
sections.summary: Scope: this standard applies to degermed maize (corn) meal and degermed maize (corn) grits milled from common maize (Zea mays L.) intended for direct human consumption and explicitly excludes many other maize products and uses (e.g., whole meal, flours, quick/hominy grits, enriched/bolted products, flakes, alkaline‑treated products, brewing adjuncts, starch/industrial uses, and animal feed).
Document identifier: CXS 155‑1985 (Standard code for degermed maize meal and grits).

sections.section: Adopted in 1985. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Revision history: the standard was adopted in 1985, revised in 1995, and amended in 2019 and 2023 (administrative metadata, not substantive requirements).
Scope details: reiterates that the standard applies to degermed maize meal and grits for direct human consumption and lists specific product types and uses that are excluded from the standard.

Product description: defines degermed maize (corn) meal and degermed maize (corn) grits as foods produced from fully mature, sound, degermed maize kernels cleaned of impurities and milled to specified fineness with bran and germ removed (grits: germ almost completely removed).

Sets out essential composition and quality factors for degermed maize meal and grits, including general safety/sensory/filth requirements and a specific maximum moisture content (15% m/m) with allowance for lower limits for certain destinations.

General quality requirements: degermed maize meal and grits must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours and living insects, and free from filth in amounts that could hazard health.

Specific quality factor: establishes a maximum moisture content (15% m/m) and notes that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations based on climate and transport/storage duration.
Moisture limit: maximum moisture content of degermed maize meal and grits is 15% m/m, with the possibility of lower limits for particular destination/climate/transport considerations.

Contaminants requirements: degermed maize meal and grits must be free from heavy metals at hazardous levels and must comply with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides and maximum limits for mycotoxins established by the CAC.
Heavy metals: the product shall be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Pesticide residues: the product must comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum residue limits established for this commodity.
Mycotoxins: the product must comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum mycotoxin limits established for this commodity.

Hygiene provisions: recommends preparing and handling the product in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene and other relevant CAC codes, requires freedom from objectionable matter to the extent possible, and mandates that when tested the product be free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbial‑origin substances in amounts that may hazard health.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969) 1  and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: Hygiene recommendation: advises that the product be prepared and handled following the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969) and other relevant CAC codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: Objectionable matter: under good manufacturing practice the product should be free from objectionable matter wherever possible.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product
sections.summary: Testing requirement: when sampled and examined by appropriate methods, the product shall be free from microorganisms, parasites, and any substances originating from microorganisms in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

Packaging requirements: packaging must protect the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities; materials must be safe and non‑reactive and not impart toxic substances or off‑odours/flavours; sacks must be clean, sturdy and properly sealed.

sections.section: 6.1 Degermed maize (corn) meal and maize (corn) grits shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Requires degermed maize (corn) meal and maize (corn) grits to be packaged in containers that safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities of the product.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Mandates that containers and packaging materials be made of substances safe and suitable for their intended use and must not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: When packaged in sacks, the sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

States that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), specific labelling provisions apply for this product; these include the prescribed product name and requirements for non-retail container information and identification linked to accompanying documents.
Requires the product name on the label to be either 'degermed maize (corn) meal' or 'maize (corn) grits'.

Permits information for non-retail containers to be on the container or in accompanying documents but requires the product name, lot identification and manufacturer/packer name and address on the container, with lot ID and name/address optionally replaced by an identification mark that is clearly identifiable with accompanying documents.

Requires use of the methods in 'Recommended methods of analysis and sampling' (CXS 234-1999) to check compliance, advises specifying appropriate limits and methods where multiple are given, and sets factor limits including ash (max 1.0% d.w.), protein (min 7.0% d.w.), crude fat (max 2.25% d.w.) and detailed granularity sieve-passage criteria for degermed maize meal and grits.
